subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board, Cards, and Tracks
- **1 board** — Main map used in both Canal and Rail eras.
- **64 Location/Industry cards** — Core deck cards tied to spaces and industry types.
- **8 Wild cards** — 4 Wild Location + 4 Wild Industry.
- **4 Player Aids** — Turn/action reminder cards.

#### Markers and Economy
- **4 VP markers** — One per player.
- **4 Income markers** — One per player.
- **67 money tokens** — Base money supply.
- **Deluxe note** — Deluxe edition uses 70 Iron Clays instead of money tokens.

#### Resources
- **30 coal cubes** — Coal market + supply resource.
- **18 iron cubes** — Iron market + supply resource.
- **15 beer barrels** — Merchant/supply beer resource.

#### Player Pieces
- **4 Player Mats** — One per player.
- **4 Character tiles** — Turn-order identifiers.
- **56 Link tiles** — 14 per colour.
- **180 Industry tiles** — 45 per colour.

#### Industry-Type Breakdown (Shown in Component Panel)
- **11 Cotton Mills** — Per colour example count.
- **7 Coal Mines** — Per colour example count.
- **4 Iron Works** — Per colour example count.
- **11 Manufacturers** — Per colour example count.
- **5 Potteries** — Per colour example count.
- **7 Breweries** — Per colour example count.

#### Merchants
- **9 Merchant Tiles** — Used to seed external markets and beer spaces.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose board side** — Place board on either day side or night side.

#### Player-Count Filtering
--- 2 Players ---
- **Card filtering** — Remove all cards showing player count greater than 2.
- **Merchant filtering** — Remove Merchant tiles showing player count greater than 2.
- **Merchant placement result** — Do not place Merchant tiles in Warrington and Nottingham.
- **Location-banner deck rule** — Blue and teal location cards are not in Draw Deck.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Card filtering** — Remove all cards showing player count greater than 3.
- **Merchant filtering** — Remove Merchant tiles showing player count greater than 3.
- **Merchant placement result** — Do not place Merchant tile in Nottingham.
- **Location-banner deck rule** — Teal location cards are not in Draw Deck.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Card filtering** — Keep all 4-player-legal cards and tiles.
- **Merchant placement result** — Place Merchants on all spaces marked for 4 players.
- **Location-banner deck rule** — All location cards are in Draw Deck.

2. **Wild cards** — Place Wild Location and Wild Industry cards face up in 2 Card Draw Areas.
3. **Draw deck** — Shuffle remaining cards and place face down in remaining Card Draw Area.
4. **Merchant draw** — Shuffle remaining Merchant tiles and place 1 face up on each valid Merchant space for your player count.
5. **Merchant beer** — Place 1 beer barrel on each beer space beside each non-blank Merchant tile.
6. **Coal market** — Fill coal market spaces with black cubes, leaving 1 of the £1 spaces open.
7. **Iron market** — Fill iron market spaces with orange cubes, leaving both £1 spaces open.
8. **General supply** — Place remaining cubes and beer beside board.
9. **Bank** — Place money beside board as Bank.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Take player mat** — Each player takes 1 Player Mat.
2. **Starting cash** — Each player takes £17 from the Bank.
3. **Choose colour** — Each player chooses 1 coloured Character tile.
4. **Links** — Place matching coloured Link tiles in your player area.
5. **Industry stacks** — Stack matching Industry tiles on mat slots (Cotton Mills, Coal Mines, Iron Works, Manufacturers, Potteries, Breweries).
6. **Tile orientation** — Place tiles with black top half face down.
7. **VP marker** — Place on 0 of Progress Track.
8. **Income marker** — Place on 10 of Progress Track.
9. **Draw hand** — Draw 8 cards into your hand.
10. **Seed discard** — Draw 1 extra card and place face down as your Discard Pile.
11. **Turn order setup** — Shuffle all players’ Character tiles together and place in random order on Turn Order Track.

- **Hidden info** — Do not reveal your hand to other players.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **Era sequence** — Play Canal Era (1770-1830) first, then Rail Era (1830-1870).
- **Turn order source** — Character tiles on Turn Order Track define acting order.
- **Normal turn size** — Players normally perform 2 actions per turn.
- **Canal opening exception** — In the first round of Canal Era, each player performs only 1 action.

#### Rounds per Era by Player Count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Rounds per era** — 10 rounds.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Rounds per era** — 9 rounds.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Rounds per era** — 8 rounds.

- **Round-end condition** — Rounds continue until both Draw Deck and players’ hands are exhausted for that era.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Set action count** — Use 2 actions this turn.
2. **Canal first-round exception** — Use only 1 action in first Canal round.
3. **Spend card per action** — Discard a card to perform each chosen action.
4. **Resolve costs** — Pay money/resources/beer required by the action.
5. **Resolve outcomes** — Apply tile placements, flips, market changes, and track updates.
6. **End turn** — Next player in turn-order track acts.

#### Era Flow
1. **Play rounds** — Continue rounds until Draw Deck and hands are exhausted for current era.
2. **Score era** — Score links and flipped industries at era end.
3. **Transition eras** — Apply Canal→Rail transition cleanup.
4. **Finish game** — After Rail era scoring, highest VP wins.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action Framework
- **Card-driven rule** — Most actions require discarding a permission card.
- **Wild flexibility** — Wild cards can substitute for location/industry permissions.
- **Repeat allowed** — You may take the same action twice if legal.

#### Build Industry
- **Card requirement** — Discard legal location/industry (or wild) card.
- **Cost payment** — Pay printed tile cost in money.
- **Placement rule** — Place industry tile in a legal location connected/allowed by rules.
- **Input resources** — Pay required coal/iron/beer from legal sources.
- **Flip check** — If placement/consumption satisfies condition, flip and gain printed benefits.

#### Build Network
- **Link placement** — Place your coloured link tile on a legal connection.
- **Canal era form** — Place canal link under Canal rules.
- **Rail era form** — Place rail link under Rail rules.
- **Cost/resources** — Pay link cost and required coal where applicable.

#### Develop
- **Upgrade action** — Spend action + required iron/card to remove lower-level tiles from mat.
- **Purpose** — Reach stronger tile levels earlier.

#### Sell
- **Market usage** — Use merchants + beer access to sell eligible goods.
- **Flip engine** — Successful selling often flips production tiles.

#### Loan
- **Immediate cash** — Take money from Bank.
- **Income penalty** — Move Income marker downward per loan rule.

#### Scout (Living Rulebook Option)
- **Card conversion** — Discard cards to gain wild flexibility.

#### Resource/Market Rules
- **Connectivity gate** — Coal, iron, and beer usage depends on legal connectivity/availability.
- **Market effect** — Market buying changes price state and can trigger indirect opponent value.

#### Hand Economy
- **Opportunity cost** — Every discarded card removes a future option.
- **Planning scope** — Hand planning must cover both Canal and Rail timelines.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 Player-Count Audit (From Provided Rulebook Text)
- **2-player references found** — Card/tile filtering, merchant placement changes, location-card exclusions, 10 rounds per era.
- **3-player references found** — Card/tile filtering, merchant placement change, location-card exclusions, 9 rounds per era.
- **4-player references found** — Full location-card set available, 8 rounds per era.
- **Fewer-players wording found** — Remove cards/merchant tiles showing counts above your player count.
- **No solo mode text found** — No solo/solitaire variant present in provided excerpt.

#### Dual-Era Structure
- **Canal era role** — Early expansion and foundational positioning.
- **Rail era role** — Late-game conversion and final scoring race.
- **Obsolescence pressure** — Not all Canal infrastructure remains equally valuable after transition.

#### Tile Flipping Economy
- **Primary value trigger** — Industries typically become high-value when flipped.
- **Interactive flips** — Opponents consuming your resources can help flip your tiles.
- **Board design goal** — Build lines that invite profitable external consumption.

#### Shared Market Dynamics
- **Coal setup signal** — Coal market starts with one £1 space open.
- **Iron setup signal** — Iron market starts with both £1 spaces open.
- **Pricing pressure** — Purchases move market state and alter later action efficiency.

#### Merchant & Beer Layer
- **Merchant function** — Merchants create demand nodes and beer access points.
- **Beer function** — Beer is a key enabler for many sales/rail conversions.

#### Turn-Order Tempo
- **Order impact** — Earlier turns can claim scarce links, markets, and merchant opportunities.
- **Late-turn risk** — High-value spots can vanish before your action window.

#### Network Envelope
- **Placement scope** — Links define where you can efficiently build and source resources.
- **Bad-network penalty** — Weak connectivity forces expensive market dependence.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **Game end** — Game ends after Rail Era scoring is completed.

#### Era Scoring Sources
- **Link VP** — Score VP for your link tiles at end of each era.
- **Industry VP** — Score VP for your flipped industry tiles at end of each era.

#### Era Sequence
- **Canal scoring** — Score after Canal rounds end.
- **Rail scoring** — Score after Rail rounds end; this determines winner.

#### VP vs Income
- **Income track** — Income affects cash flow during play.
- **Win metric** — VP marker determines winner, not income.

#### Tie-Break Procedure
- **Primary winner** — Highest VP after Rail scoring wins.
- **First tie-break** — Highest income. Second tie-break: most money remaining.
- **Final tie state** — If still tied, tied players share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Open with connectivity** — Use first links to connect likely coal/iron access and future build hubs.
- **Protect cash runway** — Starting £17 can vanish quickly; plan a proactive loan window before forced debt.
- **Flip early, not pretty** — Prioritize builds that can flip soon over attractive but stalled placements.
- **Read beer map early** — Identify which merchant beer spots enable your first sales line.
- **Exploit Canal round-1 exception** — Choose one high-leverage setup action in the 1-action opening round.

#### Player-Count Emphasis
--- 2 Players ---
- **Longer era horizon** — 10 rounds per era gives more time for setup lines to mature.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Balanced tempo window** — 9 rounds per era rewards both setup and conversion timing.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Compressed timing** — 8 rounds per era increases urgency for early flips and efficient links.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Unflippable builds** — Building industries that cannot flip before scoring wastes money and board slots.
- **Card bottleneck blindness** — Holding ideal long-term cards while lacking legal short-term plays causes dead turns.
- **Market complacency** — Repeated coal/iron market buys at rising prices quietly wreck margins.
- **Decorative links** — Links without access/scoring purpose drain action economy.
- **Late development** — Delaying Develop keeps weak tile levels too long.
- **Beer misuse** — Spending or hoarding beer without sell timing blocks your own conversions.
- **Leader tunnel vision** — Ignoring opponent rail positioning enables uncontested endgame scoring spikes.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Canal now vs Rail later** — Decide whether this action should score now or secure stronger Rail access.
- **Loan now vs income later** — Choose between immediate tempo cash and long-term income stability.
- **Producer vs converter posture** — Build resource industries for opponent consumption value or focus on self-conversion chains.
- **Flexible hand vs committed line** — Keep broad options or commit to a narrow high-payoff route.
- **Efficiency vs market pressure** — Accept short-term inefficiency when it meaningfully improves market state or denies opponents.

metadata.notes: Primary source: Brass: Birmingham rulebook text provided in prompt, including component inventory, setup sequence, player setup, rounds/eras structure, and scoring framework.
